alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier "accurately," clarifying the original claim and increasing expressed confidence in prediction quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology yields probabilistic estimates of parameters, providing information about the confidence and uncertainty related to the estimates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides probabilistic estimates of sub-surface parameters, which include information about confidence and uncertainty related to the estimates.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the probabilistic/uncertainty claim but frames it specifically for sub-surface parameters and lowers expressed certainty (high -> medium), so it is a clarification with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrated predictions consistent with time-domain reflectometry (TDR) measurements and conventional gravimetric tests.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation coefficient between predicted soil permittivity and that measured by TDR ranged from 0.84 to 0.99 for the presence of up to 10 cm of overlaying surface layer.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantified TDR correlation (and a depth bound) rather than the broader "consistent with TDR and gravimetric tests," narrowing and specifying the original consistency claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The depth of the surface layer could be predicted with reasonable accuracy using the proposed method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The depth of the surface layer could be accurately predicted for up to 10 cm, with prediction outliers reasonably identified at higher depths.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the depth-prediction claim by specifying accuracy limits (up to 10 cm) and noting outliers at greater depths, narrowing and clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides a promising approach for uncertainty-aware sub-surface parameter estimation that can enable decision-making for risk assessment.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly asserts the broader promise for decision-making or risk-assessment enablement; that higher-level contribution is not present in the published claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology was evaluated using a diverse range of experimental data collected through laboratory and field investigations.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an evaluative/limitation claim about the diversity of experimental data used, which was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel neural network-based sequence embedding technique using Poisson regression is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel neural network-based sequence embedding technique using Poisson regression is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Poisson regression is introduced by assuming the Levenshtein distance between sequences of fixed length follows a Poisson distribution.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not restate the specific modeling assumption about Levenshtein distance following a Poisson distribution, so this specific methodological assumption appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Poisson regression approximates the negative log likelihood of the chi-squared distribution and offers advancements in removing the skewness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poisson regression approximates the negative log likelihood of the chi-squared distribution and offers advancements in removing the skewness.
alignments.rationale: The technical claim about the Poisson regression approximation and skewness treatment appears verbatim and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superior performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches through comprehensive experiments on real DNA storage data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superior performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the superior-performance claim but omits the explicit mention of "comprehensive experiments on real DNA storage data," slightly generalizing/clarifying the performance claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A criterion for selecting an appropriate embedding dimension is presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new practical criterion for embedding-dimension selection that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prediction models based on the exponential moving average (EMA) outperform other simple statistical methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction models based on the exponential moving average (EMA) outperform other simple statistical methods.
alignments.rationale: This claim appears verbatim in both the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of EMA is nearly as good as that of artificial neural networks, but with dramatically lower computational requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the ELC model is nearly as good as that of artificial neural networks but with dramatically lower computational requirements.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the form of the comparison to neural networks but shifts the subject from EMA to the ELC model, revising attribution and narrowing the claim to the new model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new model called EMA linear combination (ELC) brings tangible improvements over EMA in any experimental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EMA linear combination (ELC) model brings tangible improvements over EMA in prediction accuracy under various experimental conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the ELC-improvement claim with slightly clearer wording about prediction accuracy and experimental conditions, but preserves the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The only drawback of the ELC model is a slight increase in computational complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The only drawback of the ELC model is a slight increase in computational complexity.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BOTH57M is a novel multi-modal dataset for two-hand motion generation that includes accurate motion tracking and pair-wised finger-level hand annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BOTH57M is a novel multi-modal dataset for two-hand motion generation that includes accurate motion tracking and pair-wised finger-level hand annotations.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BOTH2Hands is a strong baseline method for generating vivid two-hand motions from implicit body dynamics and explicit text prompts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BOTH2Hands is a strong baseline method for generating vivid two-hand motions from implicit body dynamics and explicit text prompts.
alignments.rationale: The baseline-method claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments and cross-validations demonstrate the effectiveness of BOTH57M and BOTH2Hands for generating convincing two-hand motions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments and cross-validations demonstrate the effectiveness of BOTH57M and BOTH2Hands for generating convincing two-hand motions.
alignments.rationale: The experimental-validation claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IndoorGNN, a Graph Neural Network-based algorithm, classifies specific locations based on RSSI values collected at those locations.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on performance, robustness, and applications of IndoorGNN but do not include the explicit methodological/contribution statement that the model classifies specific locations from RSSI, so this contribution statement from the preprint is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IndoorGNN demonstrates comparable prediction accuracy to that of the complete dataset even when tested on a restricted dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IndoorGNN demonstrates comparable prediction accuracy to that of the complete dataset when tested on a restricted dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about comparable accuracy on restricted data with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IndoorGNN outperforms state-of-the-art existing conventional and GNN-based methods for indoor localization tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IndoorGNN outperforms state-of-the-art existing conventional and GNN-based methods for indoor localization tasks.
alignments.rationale: The published claim directly matches the preprint claim about outperforming existing methods with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of IndoorGNN does not significantly decrease with a reduction in the number of available data points.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of IndoorGNN does not significantly decrease with a reduction in the number of available data points.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same conclusion about robustness to reduced data, with the same medium certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: IndoorGNN can be utilized for navigation, asset tracking, and enhancing mobile applications with location-based services.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an application-oriented claim about potential uses of IndoorGNN (navigation, asset tracking, mobile apps) that does not appear in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified model achieved higher validation accuracy and macro F1 score at both the slice and patient levels compared to our previous solution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for detecting COVID-19 from CT images demonstrates improved performance compared to the previous solution and baseline methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result of improved performance but clarifies and broadens the comparison to include baseline methods beyond the previous solution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for detecting COVID-19 from CT images includes removing the uppermost and lowermost slices and manual cropping to emphasize lung areas.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention these specific preprocessing steps, so the detailed procedural claim from the preprint is not present among the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the Xception transfer learning model contributed to the improved performance in COVID-19 diagnosis from CT images.
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights improved performance but does not attribute it explicitly to Xception transfer learning, so the specific model-contribution claim is absent in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves comparable performance to the highest-achieving methods on the COV19-CT database.
alignments.rationale: This comparative claim with top-performing methods is present in the publication but not in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a big and rigorously annotated database of over 5000 CT images supports the verification of the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about dataset scale and annotation supporting verification that was not in the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further validation studies could explore the scalability and adaptability of the developed methodologies across diverse healthcare settings.
alignments.rationale: A new limitation/suggested future work statement appears in the publication but was not in the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Investigating advanced image processing techniques could enhance the efficiency and accuracy of COVID-19 diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a forward-looking contribution about further image processing work that is not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Saturn is an innovative platform that assists Foundation Model (FM) building and its integration with IT operations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saturn is an innovative platform that assists Foundation Model (FM) building and its integration with IT operations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level contribution claim about Saturn's role in FM building and IT integration with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Saturn enables data scientists to effectively create and implement FMs while enhancing collaboration within their technical domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saturn is custom-made to meet the requirements of data scientists, enabling them to effectively create and implement FMs.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state Saturn's purpose for data scientists and enabling FM creation and deployment; the publication wording is slightly rephrased but the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Saturn streamlines and automates different stages of FM development, making it an invaluable asset for data science teams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saturn streamlines and automates different stages of FM development, making it an invaluable asset for data science teams.
alignments.rationale: The result about streamlining and automating FM development is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The white paper introduces prospective applications of generative AI models derived from FMs in the financial sector.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expected impacts of Saturn on the financial sector are discussed in this white paper.
alignments.rationale: Both describe discussion of Saturn's impacts/applications in the financial sector; wording differs slightly but the substantive conclusion is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed BC-DRL framework achieves higher security, reliability, and resource utilization efficiency than benchmark blockchain consensus protocols.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed BC-DRL framework achieves higher security, reliability, and resource utilization efficiency than benchmark blockchain consensus protocols and MEC resource allocation algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the set of baselines the framework is claimed to outperform (adding MEC resource allocation algorithms), broadening the comparative scope while preserving the core performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The constrained DRL effectively attains optimal resource allocations that adapt to dynamic DoS requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constrained DRL optimally allocates resources that adapt to dynamic denial-of-service requirements.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about constrained DRL achieving optimal, adaptive allocations for DoS scenarios with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MEC aggregated features significantly reduce the high-dimensionality input of the remaining service processing time for individual MEC requests.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this specific claim about MEC aggregated features reducing input dimensionality, so this contribution appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The low-latency reputation-based proof-of-stake consensus protocol effectively selects highly reliable blockchain-enabled BSs to securely store MEC user requests.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a new, specific claim about a reputation-based PoS consensus protocol and BS selection for secure storage of MEC requests that does not appear among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large Language Models (LLMs) significantly impact various Software Engineering tasks such as code completion, test generation, program repair, and code summarization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large Language Models (LLMs) significantly impact various Software Engineering tasks such as code completion, test generation, program repair, and code summarization.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level result about LLMs' significant impact on multiple SE tasks with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerous intricate factors can influence the outcomes of experiments involving LLMs, posing potential threats to the validity of LLM-based research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerous intricate factors can influence the outcomes of experiments involving LLMs, posing potential threats to the validity of LLM-based research.
alignments.rationale: Both versions identically state concerns about factors that threaten validity in LLM experiments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes a set of guidelines for Software Engineering researchers and Language Model providers to mitigate concerns related to LLM-based research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper proposes a set of guidelines for Software Engineering researchers and Language Model providers to mitigate concerns related to LLM-based research.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of proposing guidelines is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implications of the proposed guidelines are illustrated using existing good practices and a practical example in the context of test case generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implications of the proposed guidelines are illustrated using existing good practices followed by LLM providers and a practical example for SE researchers.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the illustration description by specifying actors (LLM providers and SE researchers) and slightly rephrases the example context, clarifying who the practices and examples target.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed artificial neural network model achieved an accuracy of 99.02% in detecting transits in light curves. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed artificial neural network model achieved an accuracy of 99.02% in detecting transits in light curves. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identical numeric accuracy claim with the same context and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated error of the model's loss function was 0.03. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated error (loss function) of the model was 0.03. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The same loss/error value and interpretation appear in the published version without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 1D convolutional neural network is a good choice for working with non-phased-folded Mandel and Agol light curves with transits. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 1D CNN is a good choice for working with non-phased-folded Mandel and Agol light curves with transits. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reduces the number of light curves that need to be visually inspected for transit-like signals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model reduces the number of light curves that need to be visually inspected for transit-like signals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model decreases the time needed for analyzing each light curve compared to traditional analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model decreases the time needed for analyzing each light curve compared to traditional analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about time savings with unchanged wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VAE-GAN outperforms other machine learning algorithms when up to 10% of the total training data is available in identification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VAE-GAN outperforms other machine learning algorithms when up to 10% of the total training data are available in identification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning based soft-failure management framework can reliably run on a fraction of available training data and identify unknown failure types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No direct equivalent appears in the publication; claims about the full framework's reliable operation and explicit unknown-failure identification are not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advanced training mechanism for the GAN shows a high F1-score for unknown spectrum identification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advanced training mechanism for the GAN shows a high F1-score for unknown spectrum identification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The high F1-score result for the GAN training mechanism is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The failure localization comparison demonstrates the advantage of a low complexity neural network in combination with a VAE over established machine learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The failure localization comparison shows the advantage of a low complexity neural network in combination with a VAE over established machine learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain-inspired computing represents a significant intersection between multimodal technology and the biomedical field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolution of brain-inspired computing can be divided into two stages: recent machine learning and current deep learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Despite advances in brain-inspired computational models, challenges remain in fully exploiting their capabilities. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides insights into possible directions for future academic research in brain-inspired computing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT into human-robot teaming environments facilitates variable autonomy through verbal communication. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A user study with 12 participants indicates that users may have preconceived expectations on how to converse with robots. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current speech detection methods are inadequate for determining the number of speakers and classifying genders due to the presence of many possible classes. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Anisotropic patch embedding (APE) is introduced to capture more discriminative features of high myopia. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A meticulous quality assessment phase refines the selection to a subset of 25 high-quality general surveys. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: NAC-TCN achieves comparable, better, or state-of-the-art performance over TCNs, TCAN, LSTMs, and GRUs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about addressing in-plane rigid-body motion with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method leverages deep neural networks to estimate motion parameters in k-space and restores degraded images to avoid hallucinations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes deep neural networks to estimate motion parameters in k-space and employs a model-based approach to restore degraded images.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution: DNN-based parameter estimation in k-space and model-based image restoration; wording is slightly different but the content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the approach achieves good generalization performance on both simulated data and in-vivo acquisitions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the approach achieves good generalization performance on both simulated data and in-vivo acquisitions.
alignments.rationale: The experimental generalization claim is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can estimate motion occurring in high spatial frequencies without requiring a motion-free reference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is capable of estimating motion occurring in high spatial frequencies without requiring a motion-free reference.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the capability to estimate high spatial frequency motion without a motion-free reference with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D Gaussian Splatting achieves a drastic reduction in storage requirements without compromising visual quality during rendering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed compact scene representation reduces storage requirements for 3D Gaussian Splatting by a factor of 17× to 42× without compromising visual quality during rendering.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the visual-quality/efficiency claim but adds a concrete numeric compression factor, clarifying and broadening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves a reduction factor of 17x to 42x in size for complex scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed compact scene representation reduces storage requirements for 3D Gaussian Splatting by a factor of 17× to 42× without compromising visual quality during rendering.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly reports the same quantitative reduction factor and couples it with the maintained visual quality; the numeric result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel highly parallel algorithm preserves the neighborhood structure of high-dimensional Gaussian parameters while arranging them into a 2D grid.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel highly parallel algorithm organizes high-dimensional Gaussian parameters into a 2D grid while preserving their neighborhood structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the algorithmic contribution verbatim in content and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The uncompressed Gaussians use the same structure as 3DGS, ensuring seamless integration with established renderers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method maintains the same structure as 3D Gaussian Splatting, ensuring seamless integration with established renderers.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert structural compatibility with 3DGS and integration with existing renderers with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The probabilistic and longitudinal machine learning framework based on multi-mean Gaussian processes can accurately predict DNA methylation status at ages 5-7 for children profiled at ages 0-4.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method can simultaneously predict methylation status at multiple genomic sites in children using methylation data from earlier ages.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the predictive capability across sites using earlier-age data but frames it more generally (multiple sites), while the preprint gave a specific age-range example.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methylation profiles predicted by multi-mean Gaussian processes can be used to estimate other phenotypes, such as epigenetic age.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted methylation profiles can be used to estimate other molecular phenotypes, such as epigenetic age, at any timepoint.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the idea that predicted methylation can estimate epigenetic age but broadens it (applicable at any timepoint), so the claim is clarified and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach encourages epigenetic studies to adopt longitudinal designs for investigating epigenetic changes during development, ageing, and disease progression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Limited longitudinal profiling of DNA methylation coupled with machine learning enables forecasting of epigenetic ageing and future health outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both statements promote longitudinal designs; the publication reframes this as enabling forecasting of ageing and health outcomes, which broadens the applied implication beyond the preprint's encouragement of longitudinal studies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed novel formulation improves upon the limitations of existing deterministic extensions of deep CCA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed novel formulation improves upon the limitations of existing deterministic extensions of deep CCA.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication sentences are effectively identical in wording and claim; no substantive change in scope or certainty is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach models the private components as conditionally independent given the common ones, leading to a more compact formulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new approach models the private components as conditionally independent given the common ones.
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the explicit consequence about leading to a more compact formulation, thereby removing an asserted benefit and narrowing the claim content while keeping the core modeling statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sufficient condition for identifying the common random factors is provided in this work.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sufficient condition for identifying the common random factors is provided in this work.
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same but the publication reports higher certainty, indicating an amplification of confidence in the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Judicious experiments with synthetic and real datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Judicious experiments with synthetic and real datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
alignments.rationale: Experimental claim is carried forward verbatim with the same scope and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VADIS system provides enhanced information access in the domain of the social sciences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VADIS system provides enhanced information access in the domain of the social sciences.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The VADIS system interlinks survey variables with scholarly publications.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can optimize high dimensional problems quickly without knowledge of the experiment itself.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum models can reach the same accuracy with less memory compared to classical models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum models can reach the same accuracy with less memory compared to classical models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum models can achieve better accuracy with the same memory compared to classical models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: NeurDB provides in-database AI-powered analytics and self-driving capabilities to improve system performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian optimization effectively explores the design space and identifies optimal parameter combinations meeting desired stress and resistance specifications. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach aims to achieve desired residual stress and sheet resistance while minimizing susceptibility to stochastic fluctuations during deposition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sputter deposition parameters influence physical properties like residual stress and resistance, which are critical for device performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
